message:
chore(beads): close bd-s170 resolver smoke-fuzz suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-rotr","title":"Conformance: direct execute pthread_thread fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for pthread_thread lifecycle fixtures so create/join/detach/self/equal cases are checked against the shared executor API directly.","status":"done","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:25:24.168249225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:43:59.028142358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","pthread","testing"]}
 {"id": "bd-rqn", "title": "Production kernel manifest: minimal required math set + compile-time gates", "description": "Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Define default production kernel (small, explainable, benchmarked).
- Add compile-time feature gates separating production vs research modules.

Acceptance:
- Default build excludes ResearchOnly modules.
- Production kernel meets strict/hardened latency budgets.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "Perf gate rerun on idle host passed (scripts/perf_gate.sh): strict deltas within threshold, hardened decide_observe delta +1.74%. However target budget checks still rely on allow_target_violation policy in perf gate, and manifest currently keeps all runtime_math modules in production with research_only_modules empty. Bead remains in_progress pending minimal production subset selection + explicit research-only migration plan.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "AzureBluff", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:11.222573367Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-19T16:39:50.848168045Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-19T16:39:50.848140263Z", "close_reason": "Completed minimal production kernel manifest partition (25 production / 44 research-only), synchronized governance/policy artifacts, and validated via runtime-math gates + rch harness tests.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alien", "critique", "math"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-1iya", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-1rxj", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-24x", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-25pf", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-2k6b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-7dw2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1136, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "Codex", "text": "Codex here. I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.", "created_at": "2026-02-12T21:12:47Z"}, {"id": 1137, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "Codex", "text": "Claimed bd-33p.2 (trace propagation + decision explainability fields). Expect small, mechanical additions in harness structured logs + ABI/membrane decision emission so failures can be traced end-to-end (trace_id/span_id/gate + decision_reason/controller/action/profile). I’ll keep changes tight and comment here if anything overlaps with your in-flight work.", "created_at": "2026-02-12T21:24:18Z"}, {"id": 1138, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "BronzePine", "text": "Progress update (BronzePine): implemented bd-rqn enabling change for production/research manifest partitioning safety gates.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/math_governance_test.rs: added manifest_union_modules() helper and switched governance coverage assertions from production-only to manifest union (production_modules U research_only_modules).\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh: updated Check 2/3 to validate against manifest union and updated pass/fail messaging accordingly.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh => PASS\n- rch doctor => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_governance_test => PASS (8/8)\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS (warnings only; existing unrelated warnings in frankenlibc-abi)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated lint debt in frankenlibc-abi (unused imports, unreachable code, dead_code, clippy style lints in locale_abi/stdio_abi/termios_abi/time_abi).\n\nRCH notes:\n- Remote offload attempted for heavy cargo commands; some runs failed open to local due worker sync instability (no-space/file-vanished on remote rsync).", "created_at": "2026-02-15T17:37:03Z"}, {"id": 1139, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "BronzePine", "text": "Additional bd-rqn progress (BronzePine): updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics.\n\nDetails:\n- Manifest extraction now normalizes both  and .\n- Admission checks run over union(manifest sets), while ADMITTED status is restricted to modules in .\n- Production-core completeness now checks specifically against production set.\n- Admission ledger now emits  and  booleans in addition to .\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py => status=pass, errors=0\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test => PASS (3/3)", "created_at": "2026-02-15T17:41:34Z"}, {"id": 1140, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "BronzePine", "text": "Correction for prior note formatting: updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics. Manifest extraction now normalizes both production_modules and research_only_modules; admission checks run over the union while ADMITTED status is restricted to production_modules. Ledger now includes in_production_manifest and in_research_only_manifest booleans. Validation: python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py -> pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test -> PASS (3/3).", "created_at": "2026-02-15T17:41:49Z"}, {"id": 1141, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "AzureBluff", "text": "Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed production-kernel partition and governance artifact alignment to unblock bd-3ot.1.

Implemented:
- tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json: moved to minimal production set (25 modules) and explicit research_only_modules (44 modules).
- tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json: updated change_gate.manifest_sha256 to b47cd1f6283dc3bb151fb0c57b7f8ef5c1b1afe526cf2b333d92eecc37ef257b, module_count=25, and summary counts to production-only.
- tests/conformance/math_retirement_policy.json: reflected completed migration state (RC-1=0, no active waivers, migration waves=0, summary updated).
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json: synchronized per-module in_production_manifest / in_research_only_manifest flags and summary counts with new manifest partition.
- Re-ran scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to regenerate:
  - tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
  - tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json

Gate/script verification (PASS):
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh

RCH validation:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_classification_matrix_test (PASS 8/8)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_retirement_test (PASS 9/9)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets started remotely and progressed through broad suite but stalled in long pthread lifecycle tests; process was terminated to avoid indefinite hang.

This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.
", "created_at": "2026-02-19T16:39:47Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-rth1", "title": "bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads", "description": "Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z", "close_reason": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 1142, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.", "created_at": "2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"}, {"id": 1143, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Codex", "text": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

Verification evidence:
- Diff in /data/projects/frankenlibc/crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs:301:
 #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
 #[allow(unsafe_code)]
 fn allocate_thread_stack(stack_size: usize) -> Result<(usize, usize, usize), i32> {
[31m-    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE.checked_add(stack_size).ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m 
     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅
-  ✅
-  ✅
-

Note: full workspace test sweeps can still hit long-running/flaky hangs in pthread lifecycle/TSD binaries under concurrent workspace load; isolated bead-scope tests above pass consistently.", "created_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:14Z"}, {"id": 1144, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Codex", "text": "Completion summary (clean):

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI TSD integration coverage in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_tsd_test.rs`:
  - `tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads`
  - `tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean`
  - serialized native-threading setup helper.

Verification:
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-core tls::tests -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean -- --nocapture` ✅

Operational note:
- Full workspace test sweeps can still exhibit intermittent long-running pthread test binaries under heavy concurrent workspace load. Bead-scope TLS tests above pass consistently.

-{"id":"bd-s170","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for resolver parsers","description":"Archetype 1 (Crash Detector) via proptest for 4 resolver parsers that consume arbitrary bytes: parse_hosts_line, parse_services_line, ResolverConfig::parse, encode_domain_name. Each parser must never panic, must terminate on any byte sequence, and must produce outputs bounded relative to the input size (no infinite expansion). One parser per commit. Oracle: crash-free + size-bounded output.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:44.821539800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:51.178310005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Each parser must never panic, must terminate on any byte sequence, and must produce outputs bounded relative to the input size (no infinite expansion). One parser per commit. Oracle: crash-free + size-bounded output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:44.821539800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.951422556Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.949455593Z","close_reason":"Added 13 smoke-fuzz proptests across 4 commits covering all 4 resolver parsers: 899230c (parse_hosts_line 3 tests), db6c4a4 (parse_services_line 3 tests), 96fae93 (ResolverConfig::parse 3 tests), 547c70e (encode_domain_name 4 tests). Each target has: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) structural invariant check, (c) where applicable, biased structured-alphabet fuzz to reach deep parser paths. ~6500 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-s39o", "title": "EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite", "description": "FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice
Complex: python3 perl node curl make tar gzip ssh openssl bash zsh vim less nano", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "assignee": "PlumCove", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.422019583Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.421726608Z", "close_reason": "All children closed: bd-s39o.1 (database clients), bd-s39o.2 (build tools), bd-s39o.3 (network tools). 61/61 core suite + 12+ complex programs pass. Smoke suite fully green at 58 passes / 0 fails / 6 skips.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "bd-s39o.1", "title": "Test and fix database clients under LD_PRELOAD (psql, mysql, sqlite3)", "description": "Test psql, mysql/mariadb client, and sqlite3 under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: socket operations, SSL/TLS (via OpenSSL), readline, locale-aware formatting, signal handling.
Run: LD_PRELOAD=... psql --version, sqlite3 --version, mysql --version
Fix any crashes, then test with actual database connections.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T05:12:52.118219604Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T05:12:50.809758463Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-s39o.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-s39o", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1337, "issue_id": "bd-s39o.1", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "sqlite3, psql, mysql not installed on this system. Cannot test. Deferred.", "created_at": "2026-03-25T05:12:52Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-s39o.2", "title": "Test and fix build tools under LD_PRELOAD (cmake, ninja, gcc)", "description": "Test cmake, ninja, and gcc under LD_PRELOAD.

Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.", "created_at": "2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
@@ -807,5 +807,5 @@
 {"id": "bd-zh1y.7.3", "title": "Wire full native getaddrinfo: numeric + hosts + DNS + AI_* flags", "description": "Combine numeric parsing + /etc/hosts + DNS stub into complete getaddrinfo. Apply AI_PASSIVE, AI_CANONNAME, AI_V4MAPPED, AI_ADDRCONFIG flags. Implement native freeaddrinfo to free our allocated addrinfo list. Remove all resolve_host_symbol_raw from resolv_abi.rs.", "acceptance_criteria": "Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw in resolv_abi.rs.
